backend/main.mo: Empty Motoko actor serving as the backend entry point with no implemented methods.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report documenting a single completed change: updating the page title in WhyILoveYou.tsx from 'Reasons Why I Love Her' to 'Reasons Why I Love You'.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-to-two sentence summaries of each frontend source file, describing their purpose, key features, and notable implementation details.
frontend/IMAGE_SLOT_INVENTORY.md: Markdown reference listing all replaceable image slots across the app (13 timeline, 5 gallery, 3 journey) with their exact file paths under frontend/public/assets/.
frontend/index.css: Base Tailwind CSS file defining OKLCH CSS custom properties for light and dark color themes including backgrounds, foregrounds, borders, charts, and sidebar variables.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML entry point titled 'Romantic Anniversary Website' that mounts the React app into a root div and loads the main TSX module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component managing client-side page routing via useState across 11 named pages, with lazy-loaded page components wrapped in Suspense and an ErrorBoundary.
frontend/src/components/AppShell.tsx: Layout wrapper rendering FloatingHearts in the background and fixed dark mode and music toggle buttons in the top-right corner using AppContext.
frontend/src/components/ErrorBoundary.ts: Class-based React ErrorBoundary that catches render errors and displays a styled fallback UI with the error message on a pink background.
frontend/src/components/FloatingHearts.t: Renders 14 deterministically positioned floating heart characters with CSS keyframe float animations, memoized to avoid re-computation on re-renders.
frontend/src/contexts/AppContext.tsx: Global React context providing dark mode toggle (synced to document class) and music play/pause toggle backed by a looping Audio element, both with silent error handling.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom React hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor via React Query, using the current Internet Identity or falling back to an anonymous actor, and invalidates dependent queries when the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides a React context and hook for Internet Identity authentication using DFINITY's AuthClient, exposing login/logout functions and status flags. Includes an InternetIdentityProvider component that persists identity across page reloads.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Placeholder file with no implementation, indicating no backend queries are needed for this frontend-only application.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet defining a romantic OKLCH color palette with CSS variables for light and dark themes, plus utility classes for glassmorphism cards, gradient buttons, and heart animations.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that renders the React app wrapped with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider into the root DOM element.
frontend/src/pages/CountdownPage.tsx: A countdown timer page targeting March 10, 2027, displaying days, hours, minutes, and seconds in glassmorphism flip units with floating heart animations and a continue button.
frontend/src/pages/CoupleQuiz.tsx: An interactive couple-themed quiz component with five questions, answer selection, score tracking, heart burst animations on answers, and a results screen.
frontend/src/pages/FinalPage.tsx: A celebratory final page with a pulsing heart, romantic message card, a celebrate button that triggers animated confetti, and a popup overlay with a restart option.
frontend/src/pages/HerJourney.tsx: Displays a three-stage photo journey (newborn, teenage, present) of the girlfriend, with each stage showing three images and a descriptive poem. Includes an error fallback component for missing images and a 'Continue' button to advance.
frontend/src/pages/HeroPage.tsx: Landing/hero page with a fade-in glass card showing the relationship start date and a tagline, plus a button to proceed to the next page. Features a pulsing heart animation and hover effects on the button.
frontend/src/pages/LockScreen.tsx: Password-protected entry screen that accepts '10032025' (the relationship date) to unlock the app, with a shake animation on wrong input and a fade-out transition on success.
frontend/src/pages/LoveLetterPage.tsx: Renders an interactive envelope that animates open to reveal a heartfelt anniversary love letter, with floating background hearts and a seal/unseal toggle. The letter text covers the couple's one-year journey including distance and reunion.
frontend/src/pages/MemoriesGallery.tsx: Photo gallery of five shared memories displayed in a grid with hover effects, floating heart overlays, and a lightbox for full-screen viewing. Falls back to a placeholder tile if an image fails to load.
frontend/src/pages/MonthPage.tsx: Detail page for a single month in the relationship timeline, showing a placeholder image, month emoji, and a text area for writing a personal memory. Includes back-to-timeline and next-month navigation buttons.
frontend/src/pages/TimelineOverview.tsx: A React page that renders a scrollable romantic timeline of monthly entries (March through March 10 one year later), each with an emoji, title, photo slot, and personal narrative text. Users can select a month or proceed to the next page.
frontend/src/pages/WhyILoveYou.tsx: A React page that cycles through 10 love reasons as animated cards, then transitions into a cinematic sequence with floating glowing hearts and an infinity symbol after the last reason, before calling onNext.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the default theme with a romantic color palette (rose, lavender, blush), elegant serif fonts, custom border radii, glow/glass box shadows, and named animations for hearts, shimmer, and cinematic effects.
project_state.json: Project state manifest (version 37) describing the multi-page romantic anniversary website's architecture, existing features, and known issues such as blank screen on load and missing asset files for gallery and HerJourney stages.
